evals.name: Quick Estimate: Pole Barn
evals.prompt: I need a quick material estimate for a pole barn. 40 feet wide, 60 feet long, 14-foot eave height, 4:12 roof pitch. Classic Rib 29ga in Arctic White. Full metal walls on all sides. One 12x14 overhead door and one 3x7 walk door. Give me panel counts, trim, and fasteners.

assertions.text: Correctly calculates roof area using 4:12 pitch multiplier (~1.05) applied to building footprint
Panel count uses 36" (3') coverage width for Classic Rib, rounds UP
Includes ridge cap, eave trim, rake trim, drip edge, corner trim, and J-channel
Subtracts door openings from wall panel counts
Includes 5% waste on panels and 10% waste on trim
Fastener count uses ~80 screws per 100 sq ft for roof, rounds to full boxes of 250
Lists all assumptions explicitly (overhang, pitch, waste factor, etc.)
Does NOT include installation labor or framing (supply only)

evals.name: Detailed Estimate: Residential Re-Roof
evals.prompt: I need a detailed material takeoff for a residential re-roof. The house is 30 feet wide by 50 feet long, 6:12 gable roof pitch, 12-inch overhangs on all sides. The homeowner wants SSQ550 standing seam in 26ga Kynar Slate Gray. There are 3 plumbing vent penetrations. No valleys. Include complete BOM with product codes.

assertions.text: Uses SSQ550 coverage width (16" not 36") for panel count — this is a standing seam panel
Applies 6:12 pitch multiplier (~1.12) to calculate actual roof area
Calculates rafter length including 12" overhang using Pythagorean theorem
Includes 3 pipe boots for the plumbing vents
Product codes use CMG color format for Kynar (CMGSG for Slate Gray)
Includes standing seam clips instead of exposed fastener screws
Mentions this is supply-only — homeowner needs to hire installer

evals.name: Quick vs Detailed Mode Awareness
evals.prompt: A contractor calls and says 'I need to know roughly how much metal I need for a 30x40 garage with a 4:12 pitch. Classic Rib, 29ga, Charcoal. Full walls, one 16x8 overhead door.' Give me a quick estimate, then tell me what additional information you'd need for a detailed engineering-grade takeoff.

assertions.text: Provides quick estimate FIRST (not a bunch of questions), respecting the 'roughly' request
Quick estimate includes approximate panel counts, basic trim list, and fastener boxes
Then lists specific questions for detailed mode: exact pitch, overhang, windows, penetrations, wainscot, soffit/fascia needs
Uses correct panel coverage (36" for Classic Rib)
Subtracts 16x8 door opening from wall panels
Includes waste factors in the quick estimate
